skill.name: nature-paper2ppt
skill.description: Build a complete but efficient Nature-style Chinese PPTX presentation from a scientific paper, preprint, PDF, article text, abstract, figure legends, or reading notes. Use this skill whenever the user asks to make slides/PPT/PPTX for journal club, group meeting, paper sharing, thesis seminar, lab meeting, department report, or academic presentation from a research paper, not only medical papers. It identifies the paper type and argument, selects only the figures needed for the story, writes Chinese slide content and speaker notes, creates the actual .pptx deck, and runs an explicit self-review/corrective revision loop focused on figure quality, text overflow prevention, and non-template visual design before delivery. Also trigger on general academic-presentation requests even without the word "Nature", such as turning a paper into slides, building an academic talk deck, conference/defense presentations, and Chinese phrasings like 论文做PPT、论文汇报、组会PPT、文献汇报、学术汇报、做幻灯片、讲paper、读书报告PPT.
skill.summary: Converts scientific papers into Chinese Nature-style PPTX decks with figure-led evidence slides.
skill.author: Community contribution, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation-only skill consisting of markdown instruction files for an AI assistant to convert scientific papers into PowerPoint presentations. No executable code, scripts, or binaries are present. The static analyzer's 222 findings are all false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' matches are markdown triple-backtick code fence delimiters (```), 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are triggered by documentation text patterns, 'Path traversal sequence' matches are legitimate relative path references (e.g., ../_shared/), 'System reconnaissance' matches are the word 'system' in legitimate documentation context, and the 'Python archive libraries' match is a legitimate zipfile reference for PPTX package validation. No prompt injection attempts were detected. The skill is safe to publish.

medium_findings.title: Markdown code fences misidentified as shell backtick execution
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 111 occurrences of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across markdown files. After reading the files, all matches are markdown triple-backtick (```) code block delimiters in fenced code blocks, not shell command substitution. The skill contains no Ruby code or shell backtick execution patterns. This is a regex/pattern false positive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations were read; matches are clearly markdown code fences, not shell execution patterns. High confidence in dismissal.

medium_findings.title: Documentation text incorrectly flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 106 occurrences of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across documentation files. No actual cryptographic code exists in the skill. The false positives are triggered by text patterns in markdown documentation discussing PPTX validation, file integrity, or similar concepts that happen to contain keywords resembling crypto terms.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code or algorithms are present in the skill; findings are documentation text pattern matches. High confidence false positive.

low_findings.title: Relative path references flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: Four 'Path traversal sequence' matches in manifest.yaml, README.md, SKILL.md, and toolchain.md are legitimate relative path references (../_shared/core/terminology-ledger.md) used to point to shared resource files in a multi-skill repository. These are not malicious path traversal attempts but standard filesystem path syntax for documentation referencing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Path references are clearly legitimate documentation cross-references in a shared file structure, not exploitation attempts.

low_findings.title: Legitimate use of zipfile for PPTX package validation
low_findings.description: The 'Python archive libraries' detection in toolchain.md references zipfile for validating PPTX package structure, which is a legitimate and necessary operation for python-pptx workflows. This is standard practice for PowerPoint file creation tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: zipfile is mentioned as part of the recommended toolchain for PPTX package validation, which is a standard, non-malicious use.

low_findings.title: Documentation word 'system' flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Thirteen 'System reconnaissance' findings are triggered by the word 'system' appearing in legitimate documentation context (e.g., 'experimental system', 'study design system', 'file system'). No actual reconnaissance activity is described or performed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All occurrences of 'system' are in academic/documentation context describing paper study systems, not system reconnaissance commands.

content.user_title: Turn research papers into Chinese Nature-style PPT decks
content.value_statement: Researchers waste hours manually building slide decks from papers for journal clubs and seminars. This skill automates the process, producing Chinese PowerPoint decks with figure-led evidence slides in Nature-style narrative arc.
actual_capabilities: Converts scientific papers into 10-16 slide Chinese PPTX presentations
Classifies paper type (discovery, methods, resource, clinical, materials, review) and selects appropriate narrative arc
Extracts key figures and tables as evidence, not decoration
Writes Chinese slide titles, bullets, captions, takeaways, and speaker notes
Generates real .pptx files using python-pptx with 16:9 layout
Runs self-review and corrective revision loop with severity-graded defect list
Produces a QA report and asset manifest alongside the deck
limitations: Default language is simplified Chinese; other languages require user request
Rendered slide previews require LibreOffice or similar headless renderer
Full supplementary material processing is opt-in, not automatic
Skill provides instructions only; execution depends on the AI assistant's tool availability

use_cases.title: Journal club presentation
use_cases.description: PhD student prepares a 15-minute journal club presentation from a Nature paper and needs a Chinese deck with key figures and speaker notes.

use_cases.title: Lab group meeting slides
use_cases.description: Research lab member summarizes a discovery paper for weekly group meeting with evidence-led slides.

use_cases.title: Thesis defense preparation
use_cases.description: PhD candidate builds a comprehensive academic deck from multiple referenced papers for thesis defense.

prompt_templates.title: Basic paper-to-PPT request
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Nature paper into a Chinese PPTX presentation for journal club: [paste PDF path or text]. Identify the paper type and create 12 slides with figure-led evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a single paper

prompt_templates.title: Specify paper type and audience
prompt_templates.prompt: This is a methods paper about a new AI tool. Create a 14-slide Chinese presentation for a 20-minute lab meeting. Emphasize problem-to-solution narrative and include the main architecture figure.
prompt_templates.scenario: User knows the paper type and presentation context

prompt_templates.title: Review paper with evidence map
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this review paper into a Chinese PPTX using evidence-map structure. Group findings by theme. Include comparison tables where the paper provides explicit data. Generate speaker notes for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-paper seminar deck
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a 20-slide Chinese academic deck combining these three papers on [topic]. Use a thematic structure with evidence from each paper. Include a synthesis slide at the end.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building comprehensive seminar presentations

output_examples.input: Convert the attached Nature paper on CRISPR gene editing into a Chinese PPTX for journal club.
output: Paper classified as discovery type with question-to-evidence arc
QA report noting all slides passed overflow checks

output_examples.input: Create a methods paper presentation about AlphaFold 3 for our lab meeting.
output: Architecture diagram and benchmark results highlighted
Speaker notes include key methodological details

best_practices: State the paper type detection to the user before designing slides so they can correct it early
Use the Terminology Ledger to keep technical terms consistent across all slides and speaker notes
Select only figures that carry the argument rather than decorating with every available figure
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate numbers, mechanisms, or figure details not present in the source paper
Do not force every paper into the same slide template; adapt the structure to the paper type
Do not deliver slides where text is expected to overflow or be clipped; split or shorten instead

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts paper PDFs, supplementary figures, Word or markdown converted text, abstract plus figure legends, structured reading notes, manually pasted article content, or an input/source.md file.

faq.question: Can it handle non-biology papers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works across life sciences, chemistry, materials science, environmental science, physics, engineering, computational biology, AI methods, and interdisciplinary research.

faq.question: How many slides does it produce?
faq.answer: Default is 12-16 slides for a 15-20 minute report. Quick requests get 10-14 slides. Detailed seminars may exceed 16 slides when justified.

faq.question: Does it generate rendered previews?
faq.answer: Only when a reliable cross-platform headless renderer like LibreOffice is available. Otherwise it performs structural QA on the PPTX package and documents the skip.

faq.question: What is the output language?
faq.answer: Default is simplified Chinese. Important technical terms, abbreviations, gene names, model names, and equations are preserved in English for accuracy.

faq.question: How does self-review work?
faq.answer: After the first draft, the skill inspects the PPTX, writes a severity-graded defect list, fixes all high-severity and reasonable medium issues, regenerates, and updates the QA report.
